summary: Three new tests cover the three TDD anchors specified in the task. TestCollectorResetClearsPIDMaps unit-validates the non-nil + len==0 contract before and after Reset, exercising direct struct field access (in-package test). TestCollectorUpdateNoEnrichment integration-tests the CollectNone path: real PG connection, asserts result.Ncols != 17 and PID maps stay empty. TestCollectorUpdateProcPidStat17Cols integration-tests the happy enrichment path: real PG connection with procpidstat view, asserts result.Ncols==17, Valid=true, len(Cols)==17. Existing buildProcPidResult unit tests (Task 03) already cover edge cases (first tick zero rates, IO unavailable, itv==0, invalid pid, two-tick deltas).

suggestions.title: Cleanup-before-swap behavior covered only at integration level
suggestions.description: The acceptance criterion 'PIDs absent from the current activity result are pruned from prev maps before each swap' is not directly asserted by a unit test. It is implicitly exercised by TestCollectorUpdateProcPidStat17Cols (which runs against real PG) and would only manifest as a bug over multiple ticks. A dedicated test could call Update() twice with different PID sets and verify prevProcPidStats contains only the second tick's overlapping PIDs. Not adding now because the logic is small and inline-readable; suggesting as a possible improvement if regressions appear.
suggestions.action: Optional. Consider adding TestCollectorEnrichmentPrunesStaleP IDs in a future round if cleanup-before-swap regresses.

suggestions.title: CollectExtra change-detection in top/stat.go not directly tested
suggestions.description: The new prevCollectExtra block adds a c.Reset() when CollectExtra changes between view switches. collectStat() is hard to unit-test without invasive refactoring (it spawns a goroutine, takes channels). The block's effect is also redundant with the fallthrough c.Reset() in the standard view-switch path, so a missing test would not cause a user-visible regression today. Manual testing via the TUI is the practical verification path.
suggestions.action: No change required for this round.

testQualityChecks.testPyramidCompliance: pass — Reset test is a fast unit test (no DB ops beyond NewCollector init); enrichment tests are integration (real PG) and target a thin happy-path slice. Pyramid shape preserved.
testQualityChecks.businessLogicCoverage: adequate — Reset semantics, no-enrichment short-circuit, and enrichment happy-path all asserted. Edge cases (first tick, itv=0, IO unavailable) covered by Task 03's buildProcPidResult tests.
testQualityChecks.edgeCaseCoverage: adequate — invalid PIDs, IO unavailable, first tick, itv==0 covered in procpidstat_test.go. Update() integrates these via buildProcPidResult.
testQualityChecks.testIsolation: pass — each test creates its own *postgres.DB connection and *Collector instance; no shared state.
testQualityChecks.mockingStrategy: n/a — tests use real PG connection (via postgres.NewTestConnect) for the integration tests; no mocks added.
testQualityChecks.assertionsQuality: pass — assertions check specific contracts (Ncols value, non-nil after Reset, Valid=true, Cols length). No 'truthy' or vague checks.
testQualityChecks.tddAnchorCompliance: pass — all three task TDD anchors implemented: TestCollectorResetClearsPIDMaps, TestCollectorUpdateNoEnrichment, TestCollectorUpdateProcPidStat17Cols. Tests fail before implementation (confirmed during TDD: build error for missing struct fields), pass after.
testQualityChecks.regressionGuards: pass — existing TestCollector_Update, TestNewCollector, TestCollector_collectDiskstats, TestCollector_collectNetdevs still pass. go test ./internal/stat/... and ./top/... pass under race detector.
